---
title: The journey of an image in a VLM
description: My understand regarding how images in VLMs are processed.
date: 2025-08-11
categories: [LLM, VLM]
---

The VLM has three pieces: the vision encoder, the pooler/projector, and the LLM.

1. The input image has size 644x476
1. The model has kernel size 14x14
1. The total number of image patches is therefore (644/14)·(476/14)=46·34=1564
1. These patches are processed into feature vectors by the vision encoder
1. The pooling/projection layer downsizes the number of image feature vectors
  - Qwen 2.5 VL combines a 2x2 block image feature vectors/patches into a single block
  - Pooling/projection is done to create a more informative token
1. The result is (46/2)·(34/2)=391
1. 391 matches the number of image tokens created by the tokenizer
